# Maintainability Checklist: Runtime Inventory and Constraints

**Purpose**: Validate that XPLAT-001 requirements and plan are maintainable for
later XPLAT consumers, stable owner handoff, concise PR review, and intentional
Markdown-only reporting.
**Created**: 2026-06-25
**Feature**: [Runtime Inventory and Constraints](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based
on feature context and requirements.

## Cross-Spec Handoff Maintainability

- [x] CHK001 Are XPLAT-002 and XPLAT-003 given non-scoring runtime and supply-chain rubrics so they can evaluate candidates without reopening XPLAT-001 scope? [Completeness, Spec FR-008, Spec FR-009, Plan Runtime Rubric Scope, Plan Supply-Chain Rubric Scope]
- [x] CHK002 Are XPLAT-005, XPLAT-006, and XPLAT-007 given owner buckets that distinguish read-only helpers, mutation helpers, and cutover guidance? [Completeness, Spec Clarifications Session 2, Data Model Owner Buckets]
- [x] CHK003 Does the quickstart require handoff notes for XPLAT-002 through XPLAT-007 in the durable report? [Traceability, Quickstart Report Target]
- [x] CHK004 Are later implementation decisions for runtime choice, supply-chain controls, runner APIs, helper ports, generated payload rebuilds, and native Windows release readiness explicitly deferred to XPLAT-002 through XPLAT-007? [Consistency, Spec FR-010, Spec FR-011, Workflow Design Concept]

## Owner Bucket Stability

- [x] CHK005 Are owner bucket slugs defined in one stable accepted set instead of free-form labels? [Clarity, Data Model Owner Buckets, Plan Inventory Method]
- [x] CHK006 Are public documentation claims, generated payload references, repository-only exclusions, historical/archive rows, and follow-up exceptions included as explicit owner buckets so non-active references do not need re-triage? [Completeness, Spec FR-006, Data Model Owner Buckets]
- [x] CHK007 Is `follow_up_spec` required for every inventory row so future XPLAT specs can consume the report without inferring ownership from prose? [Traceability, Data Model Inventory Finding]
- [x] CHK008 Are mixed-mode helpers required to split ownership by traced invocation mode at the row level? [Consistency, Spec Edge Cases, Clarifications Session 2]

## Report Reviewability

- [x] CHK009 Is the durable output constrained to one Markdown report under `docs/ai/research/` rather than scattered artifacts? [Maintainability, Spec FR-013, Research Durable Report Decision]
- [x] CHK010 Are summary counts required by classification, active runtime status, owner bucket, and follow-up spec to let reviewers scan the whole-repo result set quickly? [Measurability, Spec FR-005, Data Model Summary Counts]
- [x] CHK011 Does the plan record a reviewability budget and split decision for keeping XPLAT-001 as one inventory/rubric spike? [Maintainability, Spec Reviewability Budget, Plan Reviewability Budget]
- [x] CHK012 Is the report row schema compact enough to support stable IDs, concise evidence, classification, status, ownership, trace, rationale, and exception details without adding implementation-only fields? [Clarity, Data Model Inventory Finding]
- [x] CHK013 Are row aggregation or match-summary rules defined so 100% scan coverage can remain concise enough for PR review despite the whole-repo scan? [Resolved] [Maintainability, Spec SC-001, Plan Row Aggregation and Match-Summary Rules]

## Markdown-Only Artifact Decision

- [x] CHK014 Is the decision to avoid JSON, CSV, or contract artifacts explicitly recorded with rationale? [YAGNI, Research Markdown Tables Decision, Plan Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK015 Does the plan explain that no `contracts/` artifact is planned because XPLAT-001 defines a human-reviewable schema and rubrics rather than an API or machine-checked interchange format? [Clarity, Plan Structure Decision]
- [x] CHK016 Is future machine-readable output deferred until a later spec proves an automation benefit? [Maintainability, Research Markdown Tables Decision]
- [x] CHK017 Are exact scan commands required in the report so Markdown remains reproducible enough without a durable machine-readable scan artifact? [Traceability, Plan Inventory Method, Verification Plan]

## Static Verification Maintainability

- [x] CHK018 Are verification steps limited to static repo-local checks that later maintainers can rerun without native Windows UAT or runtime probes? [Maintainability, Spec FR-012, Plan Verification Plan]
- [x] CHK019 Are remediation paths defined for uncovered scan results, stale traces, false active-blocker promotion, generated payload source mismatch, spec-index drift, and diff hygiene? [Maintainability, Plan Static Verification Failure Remediation]
- [x] CHK020 Does the rollback guidance remain file-level because XPLAT-001 changes no installed runtime behavior? [Simplicity, Plan Review Packet Notes]

## Re-run After Remediation

- [x] CHK021 Does SC-001 now define individual and aggregate/match-summary representation without weakening 100% scan coverage? [Resolved, Spec SC-001]
- [x] CHK022 Does the plan define aggregation eligibility, required aggregate-row fields, split conditions, and match-count reconciliation? [Resolved, Plan Row Aggregation and Match-Summary Rules]
- [x] CHK023 Does the aggregation rule preserve owner bucket, follow-up spec, proof state, invocation mode, source-of-truth boundary, and exception rationale boundaries? [Consistency, Plan Row Aggregation and Match-Summary Rules]
- [x] CHK024 Does the remediation preserve the Markdown-only decision and avoid introducing JSON, CSV, contracts, or runtime implementation work? [YAGNI, Plan Row Aggregation and Match-Summary Rules]
